sanskrit:
न हि ज्ञानेन सदृशं पवित्रमिह विद्यते।
तत्स्वयं योगसंसिद्धः कालेनात्मनि विन्दति॥
transliteration: na hi jïänena sadåçaà pavitram iha vidyate / tat svayaà yoga-saàsiddhaù kälenätmani vindati
literal_meaning: Certainly nothing as sanctifying as knowledge exists here; that very knowledge, perfected through yoga, is found in the self in due time.
contextual_meaning: Self-knowledge is the ultimate inner cleanser; when practice matures, clarity about who one really is emerges naturally and brings unshakable peace.
psychological_state: Post-insight calm; the mind senses that sustained practice will dissolve inner contamination and reveal stable self-acceptance.
trigger_scenario: After months of therapy and daily meditation, a client notices that shame attacks no longer dominate and feels an effortless ease inside.
anchor_text: Self-knowledge purifies; steady practice reveals your true self and lasting peace.
